proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the cohort-by-period average treatment effect on the treated under staggered adoption when the no-anticipation assumption is relaxed to bounded anticipation k periods before adoption with a covariate-conditional anticipation magnitude bound, recovering Callaway-Sant'Anna and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille at the trivial bound
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis, which gives staggered switch-on coefficient decompositions under exact no-anticipation-style history conventions, and q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends, which concerns aggregate pretrend-summary sufficiency/nonsharpness. The gap is that this proposal is a PartialID-style sharp support interval for bounded anticipation boxes, including comparison-cohort anticipation contamination and pooled signed coefficients in aggregates, rather than a point-ID switch-on decomposition or latent pretrend-summary result.","published_axis":"Closest published results remain CallawaySantAnna2021 and DCDH2020 for no-anticipation point-ID benchmarks, RambachanRoth2023 for support-function sensitivity logic, and AugustinGutknechtLiu2025 for anticipation/misclassification bias. The gap is the covariate-conditional staggered-adoption anticipation-bias operator with normalized comparison weights, sharp box support, and shared-coordinate aggregate pooling; those papers do not state this nonparametric bounded-anticipation identified set."}
seed_list: Prior work CallawaySantAnna2021 has established point identification after choosing a limited anticipation horizon. Our seed conjectures that a nonzero covariate-conditional magnitude bound yields a sharp interval rather than a shifted point estimand, because not-yet-treated controls inside their own anticipation windows add signed support terms.

Prior work AugustinGutknechtLiu2025 would predict that anticipation is handled by modified estimators or diagnostic tests. Our seed conjectures that sharp nonparametric bounds are available without modeling the anticipation process, because the contamination is a finite linear image of covariate-conditional boxes.

Prior work PiccininniTchetgenStensrud2025 argues that no-anticipation is intervention-ambiguous. Our seed conjectures that once the anticipated adoption cohort is indexed explicitly, ambiguity turns into a testable support-function width rather than a definitional objection.

Prior work RambachanRoth2023 establishes support-function intervals for parallel-trends violations. Our seed conjectures that staggered anticipation has a different shared-coordinate geometry, because the same anticipation coordinate can enter multiple cohort-period targets with opposite signs.

Prior work DCDH2020 has established heterogeneity-robust DID estimands under no-anticipation-style conditions. Our seed conjectures that their switcher/non-switcher weights recover a zero-width corner of a wider bounded-anticipation interval, because switchers and non-switchers can both be anticipatory.

Prior work SunAbraham2021 has established that event-study coefficients can be contaminated by heterogeneous treatment effects. Our seed conjectures that bounded anticipation makes some lead contamination target-relevant rather than diagnostic noise, because a near-adoption lead is a latent pre-treatment causal distortion entering the ATT contrast.

Prior work BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 has established robust imputation estimators under no anticipation. Our seed conjectures that imputation from near-adoption untreated cells is sharply partially identified under magnitude bounds, because the base cell can be a bounded anticipatory potential outcome rather than a clean untreated observation.

Prior work RothSantAnnaBilinskiPoe2023 recommends sensitivity analysis in modern DID practice. Our seed conjectures that anticipation sensitivity should be reported as cohort-risk-set support width, because comparison cohorts inside the anticipation horizon create observable design-dependent interval width.

Prior work ManskiPepper2018 uses bounded-variation assumptions to cope with ambiguity. Our seed conjectures that bounded anticipation in staggered DID has sharper covariate-conditional endpoints than generic trend-variation bounds, because adoption timing fixes which latent coordinates can enter each contrast.

Prior work GoodmanBacon2021 decomposes timing-variation DID into weighted 2x2 comparisons. Our seed conjectures that each 2x2 component has a separate anticipation support width, because later-treated controls may be untreated but not unanticipated.

Prior work CallawaySantAnna2021's did workflow allows an anticipation parameter. Our seed conjectures that keeping near-adoption cells with a magnitude bound is not equivalent to increasing the anticipation parameter, because exclusion discards information while bounded anticipation preserves it as an interval.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CallawaySantAnna2021 has established point identification after choosing a limited anticipation horizon. Our seed conjectures that a nonzero covariate-conditional magnitude bound yields a sharp interval rather than a shifted point estimand, because not-yet-treated controls inside their own anticipation windows add signed support terms.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Identifies group-time ATT in staggered DID under conditional parallel trends and limited anticipation handled by a known horizon.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; not blocked because nonzero bounds introduce signed comparison-cohort support terms absent from the point-ID theorem.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from CallawaySantAnna2021 because their anticipation parameter already handles anticipation.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their parameter restores point ID by moving/excluding periods; it does not give sharp bounds when anticipatory periods are retained with nonzero magnitude bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AugustinGutknechtLiu2025 would predict that anticipation is handled by modified estimators or diagnostic tests. Our seed conjectures that sharp nonparametric bounds are available without modeling the anticipation process, because the contamination is a finite linear image of covariate-conditional boxes.

anchor_paper.author: Augustin, Gutknecht, and Liu
anchor_paper.claim: Studies staggered adoption DID with anticipation and misclassification, showing standard estimators are biased and proposing modified estimators/tests.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/impossibility adjacent; not blocked because the target is a positive sharp-bound theorem for a named published anticipation setting, not a strawman refutation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from AugustinGutknechtLiu2025 because they already analyze anticipation bias.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their abstracted contribution is bias correction/testing under misspecification, not sharp covariate-conditional nonparametric support intervals.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work PiccininniTchetgenStensrud2025 argues that no-anticipation is intervention-ambiguous. Our seed conjectures that once the anticipated adoption cohort is indexed explicitly, ambiguity turns into a testable support-function width rather than a definitional objection.

anchor_paper.author: Piccininni, Tchetgen Tchetgen, and Stensrud
anchor_paper.claim: Argues that no-anticipation in DID is ambiguous because the intervention being anticipated is often not formalized.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Disputed terminology; not blocked because it yields a concrete finite identified-set statement rather than a redefinition only.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from PiccininniTchetgenStensrud2025 because explicit interventions already fix anticipation.
seed_details.rebuttal: Explicit interventions fix meaning but do not compute the sharp ATT interval under magnitude-bounded anticipation.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RambachanRoth2023 establishes support-function intervals for parallel-trends violations. Our seed conjectures that staggered anticipation has a different shared-coordinate geometry, because the same anticipation coordinate can enter multiple cohort-period targets with opposite signs.

anchor_paper.author: Rambachan and Roth
anchor_paper.claim: Provides robust DID/event-study inference when post-treatment violations of parallel trends are restricted relative to pretrends.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method M from setting S1 to S2; not blocked because shared anticipation coordinates change aggregate interval width via sign pooling, not a routine application.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from RambachanRoth2023 because any linear sensitivity set has a support function.
seed_details.rebuttal: The general convex-analytic template is known, but the staggered anticipation operator and shared-coordinate recovery of CS/DCDH corners are not named there.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DCDH2020 has established heterogeneity-robust DID estimands under no-anticipation-style conditions. Our seed conjectures that their switcher/non-switcher weights recover a zero-width corner of a wider bounded-anticipation interval, because switchers and non-switchers can both be anticipatory.

anchor_paper.author: de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille
anchor_paper.claim: Shows TWFE weights can be negative under heterogeneous treatment effects and proposes an alternative estimator.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; not blocked because the zero-bound recovery plus nonzero-bound interval identifies a new anticipatory contamination term in a named estimator workflow.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DCDH2020 because their weights can be applied to any cell-level bias term.
seed_details.rebuttal: Applying weights is routine; the gap is the sharp bounded-anticipation identified set and its recovery of their estimand at the trivial bound.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SunAbraham2021 has established that event-study coefficients can be contaminated by heterogeneous treatment effects. Our seed conjectures that bounded anticipation makes some lead contamination target-relevant rather than diagnostic noise, because a near-adoption lead is a latent pre-treatment causal distortion entering the ATT contrast.

anchor_paper.author: Sun and Abraham
anchor_paper.claim: Shows conventional event-study coefficients can be contaminated by treatment effects from other relative periods under heterogeneous effects.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition; not blocked because the proposed mechanism is anticipation distortion entering ATT bounds, not another lead/lag contamination decomposition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from SunAbraham2021 because they already explain contaminated leads.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their contamination is from heterogeneous treatment-effect weighting; this seed treats pre-adoption anticipation as a bounded latent causal term in the target contrast.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 has established robust imputation estimators under no anticipation. Our seed conjectures that imputation from near-adoption untreated cells is sharply partially identified under magnitude bounds, because the base cell can be a bounded anticipatory potential outcome rather than a clean untreated observation.

anchor_paper.author: Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess
anchor_paper.claim: Derives robust and efficient imputation estimators for event-study designs under no anticipation and model restrictions on untreated outcomes.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens assumption X; not blocked because weakening no anticipation creates a sharp interval for imputation bias rather than a broader point-ID class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from their imputation residual algebra because anticipation is just an omitted residual.
seed_details.rebuttal: Residual algebra gives bias, not a sharp support interval under covariate-conditional magnitude restrictions.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RothSantAnnaBilinskiPoe2023 recommends sensitivity analysis in modern DID practice. Our seed conjectures that anticipation sensitivity should be reported as cohort-risk-set support width, because comparison cohorts inside the anticipation horizon create observable design-dependent interval width.

anchor_paper.author: Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe
anchor_paper.claim: Synthesizes recent DID methods and gives practitioner recommendations, including sensitivity analysis.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - survey/open-practice phenomenon; not blocked because it proposes a theorem-level design diagnostic for a named sensitivity gap.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is too practical and follows from existing sensitivity-analysis advice.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed object is a formal interval-width functional, not a recommendation to run sensitivity checks.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ManskiPepper2018 uses bounded-variation assumptions to cope with ambiguity. Our seed conjectures that bounded anticipation in staggered DID has sharper covariate-conditional endpoints than generic trend-variation bounds, because adoption timing fixes which latent coordinates can enter each contrast.

anchor_paper.author: Manski and Pepper
anchor_paper.claim: Uses bounded-variation assumptions to obtain partial identification under ambiguity in policy evaluation.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method M from setting S1 to S2; not blocked because adoption timing creates signed staggered risk-set coordinates absent from generic bounded-variation DID.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Manski-Pepper because bounded variables always yield interval endpoints.
seed_details.rebuttal: Generic endpoints do not identify the staggered comparison-cohort anticipation operator or the CS/DCDH zero-bound recovery.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GoodmanBacon2021 decomposes timing-variation DID into weighted 2x2 comparisons. Our seed conjectures that each 2x2 component has a separate anticipation support width, because later-treated controls may be untreated but not unanticipated.

anchor_paper.author: Goodman-Bacon
anchor_paper.claim: Decomposes DID with variation in treatment timing into weighted two-by-two comparisons.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition; not blocked because anticipation changes the causal status of comparison cells, not just the decomposition interpretation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the Bacon decomposition by adding bias terms to each 2x2 comparison.
seed_details.rebuttal: Adding bias terms is algebraic; the research kernel is the sharp support width and shared-coordinate aggregation across comparisons.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CallawaySantAnna2021's did workflow allows an anticipation parameter. Our seed conjectures that keeping near-adoption cells with a magnitude bound is not equivalent to increasing the anticipation parameter, because exclusion discards information while bounded anticipation preserves it as an interval.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of Econometrics and did package documentation
anchor_paper.claim: The group-time ATT workflow includes an anticipation horizon convention for pre-treatment periods.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; not blocked because it contrasts exclusion versus retention with sharp interval information, not merely changing k.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an implementation detail of the did package rather than a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The theorem concerns the identified set under two different information regimes: excluded cells versus bounded retained cells.

proposed_from.literature_map: CallawaySantAnna2021 (Journal of Econometrics, DOI 10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001) identifies group-time ATT under conditional parallel trends and limited anticipation by choosing a known clean horizon; open tension: it gives point ID after excluding/relocating anticipation periods, not sharp bounds under nonzero anticipation magnitudes. DCDH2020 (AER, DOI 10.1257/aer.20181169) shows TWFE can be a signed weighted sum of group-period effects and proposes a robust alternative; tension: no-anticipation remains a point-ID condition. GoodmanBacon2021 (Journal of Econometrics) decomposes timing-variation DID into weighted 2x2 comparisons; phenomenon: every comparison cell can be contaminated if about-to-be-treated controls anticipate. SunAbraham2021 (Journal of Econometrics) shows event-study leads/lags are contaminated under heterogeneous effects; tension: preperiod coefficients are not simple placebo objects. BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 (Review of Economic Studies) gives robust imputation event-study estimators under no anticipation; gap: imputation base cells may themselves be anticipatory. RambachanRoth2023 (Review of Economic Studies, DOI 10.1093/restud/rdad018) gives partial-ID intervals for parallel-trends violations; gap: their support-function logic is not specialized to staggered anticipation risk sets. RothSantAnnaBilinskiPoe2023 (Journal of Econometrics, DOI 10.1016/j.jeconom.2023.03.008) synthesizes modern DID and recommends sensitivity analysis; open problem: named sensitivity targets for anticipation. PiccininniTchetgenStensrud2025 (arXiv:2507.12891) argues no-anticipation is ambiguous unless the intervention is specified; gap: no sharp bounded-anticipation interval. AugustinGutknechtLiu2025 (arXiv:2507.20415) studies staggered adoption with anticipation and misclassification and shows standard estimators are biased; gap: modified estimators/tests rather than covariate-conditional sharp nonparametric bounds. Manski2003 and ManskiPepper2018 supply partial-ID and bounded-variation logic; tension: bounded-variation assumptions have not been turned into staggered-DID anticipation-cell support functions.
